human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for electronics-retail-specific analytics boundaries, metric-definition rigor, omnichannel source reconciliation, promo and refund caveat handling, fraud and safety escalation behavior, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to electronics-retail analytics, metric-definition logic, reconciliation notes, caveat design, and disclosure-safe escalation rather than generic BI work.

Authority sources cover FTC order, warranty, pricing-claim, and fraud guidance, NIST price-verification standards, California repair-operating guidance, CPSC recall guidance, GS1 identity standards, and SEC KPI disclosure guidance.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope analysis, metric ambiguity, stale and conflicting data, unsupported causal and disclosure claims, high-volume queue analysis, customer exception escalation, pricing-override boundaries, fraud and safety-sensitive patterns, missing facts, and tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/consumer-electronics-and-appliance-retail-and-service/electronics-retail-analytics-specialist/ so it remains distinct from guest services, pricing, fraud/loss-prevention, operations, repair intake, and warranty fulfillment lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated electronics retail analytics specialist for metric-definition, omnichannel retail reporting, refund and service caveat design, source reconciliation, and safe escalation.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is promoted separately.
